verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is a soluble protein lacking a transmembrane domain, localized primarily to the acrosome (intracellular compartment) in spermatozoa. While it participates in sperm-egg fusion recognition complexes and may transiently associate with surface IZUMO family members, it does not reach the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane via its own mechanism (no TM domain, no GPI anchor, no direct lipidation). Surface exposure, if any, would be through non-covalent, transient recruitment to other surface proteins rather than stable, autonomous anchoring. The protein fails the wash test—it is not a stably membrane-anchored partner but rather a soluble component of intracellular complexes.
